raw.objective: “to pinpoint the patterns of variability in the disposition of phenobarbital in neonates and infants, and to identify patient characteristics associated with inter-individual variability”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, GA, PNA, post-conceptional age, SEX, PHB concentration Included: WT, PNA, PHB concentration on CL, WT on Vd
raw.observations: The study expanded their initial 2005 model for neonates and infants by included a nonlinear relationship with PHB concentrations. The nonlinear relationship has an impact in dosing and should be accounted for accordingly.
raw.structure: 1 compartment with first order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: If(CONC<50) ECONC=1 Else ECONC=CONC**theta5 CL/F=(theta1*WT+theta4*PNA)*ECONC*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta2*WT*(1+eta2) KA=theta3 F1=1 Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) CONC is phenobarbital concentration in mcg/mL (or mg/L)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.00595 theta2=1.01 theta3=50 theta4=0.00141 theta5=-0.221 omega1=0.0676 omega2=0.375 sigma1=0.0506

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with first order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: If(CONC<50) ECONC=1 Else ECONC=CONC**theta5 CL/F=(theta1*WT+theta4*PNA)*ECONC*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta2*WT*(1+eta2) KA=theta3 F1=1 Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) CONC is phenobarbital concentration in mcg/mL (or mg/L)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to pinpoint the patterns of variability in the disposition of phenobarbital in neonates and infants, and to identify patient characteristics associated with inter-individual variability”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, GA, PNA, post-conceptional age, SEX, PHB concentration Included: WT, PNA, PHB concentration on CL, WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
